revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kenemu]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''Der Dunkle'' }} '''Kenemu''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste, wobei sich der Name des Dekans von den lichtschwachen [[Stern]]en ableitet und diese Himmelsregion als ''dunkler Fleck'' erscheint. Als auffälligste Sterne kommen [[Unuk]] im [[Sternbild]] [[Schlange (Sternbild)|Schlange]] sowie [[Hydor]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]] in Frage. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] nahm Kenemu in der Sethos-Schrift den 17. Platz ein; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] wurde Kenemu als 16. Dekan geführt, dessen [[heliakisch]]er Aufgang für den [[Achet#Datierungen|6. Achet II]] angesetzt war. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 91. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''Der Dunkle'' }} '''Kenemu''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste, wobei sich der Name des Dekans von den lichtschwachen [[Stern]]en ableitet und diese Himmelsregion als ''dunkler Fleck'' erscheint. Als auffälligste Sterne kommen [[Unuk]] im [[Sternbild]] [[Schlange (Sternbild)|Schlange]] sowie [[Hydor]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]] in Frage. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repäsentierte Kenemu am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 16. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|6. Achet II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 91. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are {{ill|Unukalhai|de|Unukalhai}} in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:ケネム]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Kenemu]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are {{ill|Unukalhai|de|Unukalhai}} in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:ケネム]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Kenemu]] [[de:Kenemu]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are {{ill|Unukalhai|de|Unukalhai}} in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:ケネム]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kenemu]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[en:draft:Kenemu]] [[de:Kenemu]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kenemu]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kenemu]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kenemu]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Kenemu]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are {{ill|Unukalhai|de|Unukalhai}} in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kenemu]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:ケネム]] [[en:draft:Kenemu]] [[de:Kenemu]] [[sn:Kenemu]] [[sn:enemu]]

''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kenemu]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:ケネム]] [[en:draft:Kenemu]] [[de:Kenemu]] [[sn:Kenemu]] [[sn:enemu]]

''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft}}s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the {{ill|Serpens (constellation)|de|Schlange (Sternbild)|12=draft}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)|12=draft}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Kenemu]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] }} [[sn:Kenemu]] [[ja:ケネム]] [[en:draft:Kenemu]] [[de:Kenemu]] [[sn:enemu]] [[sn: failed]]

Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint [[:en:Star|Star]]s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the [[:en:Serpens|Serpens (constellation)]] constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|qq=}} in the [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = V31:n:n:n N14-N33-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Kenemu
''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint [[:en:Star|Star]]s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the [[:en:Serpens|Serpens (constellation)]] constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|qq=}} in the [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Kenemu|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Kenemu]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Kenemu]] }} [[sn:Kenemu]] [[ja:ケネム]] [[de:Kenemu]] [[sn:enemu]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint [[:en:Star|Star]]s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the [[:en:Serpens|Serpens (constellation)]] constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|qq=}} in the [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Kenemu|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Kenemu]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Kenemu]] }} [[sn:Kenemu]] [[ja:ケネム]] [[de:Kenemu]] [[sn:enemu]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint [[:en:Star|Star]]s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the [[:en:Serpens|Serpens (constellation)]] constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} in the [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|WD=Q2005559}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Kenemu|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ケネム]] [[de:Kenemu]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q1738727}} [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Knmw''
''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint [[:en:Star|Star]]s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the [[:en:Serpens|Serpens]] constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} in the [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|WD=Q2005559}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|WD=Q770568}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''The Dark Ken-Stars'' }} '''Kenemu''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). The name of the decan is derived from its faint [[:en:Star|Star]]s, and this celestial region appears as a "dark spot." The most prominent stars associated with this decan are [[:en:Alpha_Serpentis|Alpha Serpentis]] in the [[:en:Serpens|Serpens]] constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} in the [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|WD=Q2005559}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|WD=Q770568}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|WD=Q770568}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The most prominent stars associated with this decan are {{ill|Alpha_Serpentis|en|Alpha_Serpentis|WD=Q1333626|lt=Alpha Serpentis|1=Alpha Serpentis}} in the {{ill|Serpens|en|Serpens|WD=Q8910|lt=Serpens|1=Serpens}} constellation and {{ill|Hydor|de|Hydor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydor|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} in the {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|en|Aquarius (constellation)|WD=Q10576|lt=Aquarius|1=Aquarius (constellation)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Kenemu represented the 16th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=6th Achet II|de|6. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|WD=Q770568|1=Season of the Inundation}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''.
